exampleSentencesTranslated: I just now ate, so I don't want to eat anything more.
I just now received your message a moment ago.
I just now got home from school when it started raining heavily.
notes_md:
**vừa** usually means **just now / a short time ago** for something that happened very recently. It goes before the verb.


It can also mean **both … and …** when repeated.  

Another common meaning is **just enough / fitting exactly** (size, amount, level). It can describe size, time, temperature, money, etc.

Strong memory link: think of *vừa* as “fresh + fitting” — either freshly happened or fitting just right.

exampleSentences: Anh vừa ăn cơm xong nên chưa muốn ăn thêm gì nữa.
Chị vừa nhận được tin nhắn của bạn lúc nãy.
Em vừa đi học về thì trời bắt đầu mưa rất to.

translation.en: She just bought a new car.

definitions.es: indicando que algo acaba de suceder o está sucediendo al mismo tiempo.
definitions.en: indicating that something has just happened or is happening at the same time.
definitions.vi: chỉ ra rằng một điều gì đó vừa xảy ra hoặc đang xảy ra cùng một lúc.
